questions.question: Why is anomaly detection typically framed as an unsupervised problem rather than classification?
questions.questionZh: 为什么异常检测（Anomaly Detection）通常被定义为无监督学习（Unsupervised Learning）问题，而不是分类（Classification）问题？
options: Anomaly detection does not require any data
Labeled anomalies are extremely rare, and novel anomaly types differ from previously seen ones
Supervised classification is always less accurate
Anomaly detection only works on time series data
optionsZh: 异常检测（Anomaly Detection）无需任何数据。
标注异常（Labeled Anomalies）极为罕见，且新型异常类型（Novel Anomaly Types）与以往观测到的异常存在差异。
监督分类（Supervised Classification）的准确率始终较低。
异常检测（Anomaly detection）仅适用于时间序列数据（time series data）。
questions.explanation: Anomalies are rare (often <0.1% of data), so there are too few labeled examples to train a classifier. Also, future anomalies may be of types never seen before. Modeling 'normal' and flagging deviations handles both problems.
questions.explanationZh: 异常（Anomalies）极为罕见（通常占比不足数据的 0.1%），因此缺乏足够的标注样本（labeled examples）来训练分类器（classifier）。此外，未来出现的异常可能属于前所未见的新类型。通过对“正常”模式进行建模并标记偏离（deviations）行为，即可同时解决这两个问题。

questions.question: A temperature of 90F is normal in summer but anomalous in winter. What type of anomaly is this?
questions.questionZh: 夏季90华氏度的气温属于正常现象，但在冬季则属于异常（anomaly）。这属于哪种类型的异常？
options: Point anomaly
Contextual anomaly
Collective anomaly
Statistical anomaly
optionsZh: 点异常 (Point anomaly)
上下文异常 (Contextual anomaly)
集体异常 (Collective anomaly)
统计异常 (Statistical Anomaly)
questions.explanation: A contextual anomaly is a value that is unusual given its context (time, location). The value itself might be normal in a different context. Same data point, different interpretation based on surrounding conditions.
questions.explanationZh: 上下文异常（Contextual Anomaly）是指在特定上下文（如时间、位置）下显得异常的值。该值本身在其他上下文中可能属于正常范围。相同的数据点，会因其周围条件的不同而得到不同的判定。

questions.question: The Z-score method flags points more than 3 standard deviations from the mean. When does this approach fail?
questions.questionZh: Z分数（Z-score）方法会标记出偏离均值（mean）超过3个标准差（standard deviation）的数据点。该方法在什么情况下会失效？
options: When the data is perfectly normally distributed
When the data is multimodal, skewed, or when outliers in the training data inflate the mean and std
When there are exactly 3 anomalies in the dataset
When the features are standardized
optionsZh: 当数据完全服从正态分布（Normal Distribution）时
当数据呈多模态（multimodal）或偏态（skewed），或训练数据（training data）中的异常值（outliers）拉高均值（mean）与标准差（std）时
当数据集 (dataset) 中恰好包含 3 个异常值 (anomalies) 时
当特征 (features) 被标准化 (standardized) 时
questions.explanation: Z-score assumes a single Gaussian distribution. It fails on multimodal data (multiple clusters), skewed distributions, and when outliers in training data shift the mean/std, making real anomalies harder to detect.
questions.explanationZh: Z分数 (Z-score) 假设数据服从单一的高斯分布 (Gaussian distribution)。它在处理多峰数据 (multimodal data)（即多个簇）和偏态分布 (skewed distributions) 时会失效；此外，当训练数据中的异常值 (outliers) 导致均值/标准差 (mean/std) 发生偏移时，也会使真实的异常 (anomalies) 更难被检测出来。

questions.question: How does Isolation Forest detect anomalies differently from distance-based methods?
questions.questionZh: 孤立森林（Isolation Forest）与基于距离的方法（distance-based methods）在检测异常（anomalies）方面有何不同？
options: It uses neural networks instead of trees
It isolates points using random splits; anomalies require fewer splits to isolate because they are few and different
It computes distances to every other point in the dataset
It only works on text data
optionsZh: 它采用神经网络 (Neural Networks) 而非树模型 (Tree Models)。
它通过随机划分（random splits）来隔离数据点；由于异常点（anomalies）数量较少且与正常数据差异显著，因此只需较少的划分次数即可将其隔离。
它计算数据集 (dataset) 中每个点与其他所有点之间的距离。
它仅适用于文本数据 (text data)。
questions.explanation: Isolation Forest randomly partitions data with tree splits. Anomalies, being rare and different, end up isolated (in their own leaf) with fewer splits. Short average path length = more anomalous.
questions.explanationZh: 孤立森林（Isolation Forest）通过树的分裂操作随机划分数据。异常点（Anomalies）由于数量稀少且特征迥异，通常只需较少的分裂次数就会被隔离至独立的叶子节点中。平均路径长度越短，表示数据的异常程度越高。

questions.question: You build both an unsupervised anomaly detector and a supervised fraud classifier. When should you prefer the unsupervised approach?
questions.questionZh: 你同时构建了一个无监督异常检测器（Unsupervised Anomaly Detector）和一个监督欺诈分类器（Supervised Fraud Classifier）。在什么情况下应优先选择无监督方法（Unsupervised Approach）？
options: Always -- unsupervised is always better for anomaly detection
When you need to detect novel fraud patterns that differ from historical labeled examples
When you have millions of labeled fraud examples
When you only care about precision, not recall
optionsZh: 始终如此——无监督学习（Unsupervised Learning）在异常检测（Anomaly Detection）中始终更具优势。
当您需要检测与历史标注样本（historical labeled examples）不同的新型欺诈模式（novel fraud patterns）时
当你拥有数百万标注的欺诈样本（labeled fraud examples）时
当您只关注精确率（Precision），而不关心召回率（Recall）时
questions.explanation: Supervised classifiers only catch fraud types present in training data. Unsupervised detectors flag any deviation from normal, catching novel fraud schemes. The tradeoff is higher false positive rate.
questions.explanationZh: 监督分类器 (Supervised Classifiers) 仅能识别训练数据 (Training Data) 中已有的欺诈类型。无监督检测器 (Unsupervised Detectors) 则会标记任何偏离正常模式的行为，从而捕获新型欺诈手段。其代价是误报率 (False Positive Rate) 会更高。
